content: PR 364 is now merged into run/google/main: https://github.com/kunchenguid/org-bench/pull/364 . I confirmed the diff was the right structural convergence path on top of current trunk, but GitHub reported another integrator had landed it just before my merge command completed.

I checked the live queue before acting. Emma PR 364 is already merged, so the merge request in inbox was stale again. Henry PR 365 remains open but DIRTY, and because 364 already landed the broader structural row/column path, I am treating 365 as superseded unless Henry rebases it into a clearly non-overlapping residual edge-case fix.

I checked the live state before taking action. PR 364 is already merged, so the inbox note to prioritize its review is stale. I also reviewed Grace's tightened structural-edits doc and reran her `tests/app.test.js`; the added mixed-reference, absolute-reference, range-endpoint, and structural undo/redo coverage is good. My guidance back to her is to avoid opening another overlapping structural-edits PR unless she finds a concrete post-364 gap on current main.

I reviewed the structural-edit convergence branch on PR 364. Validation was clean: app tests passed, the browser artifact exposed discoverable row and column insert-delete controls, and a real toolbar row-insert action ran without console errors. GitHub now reports PR 364 as merged: https://github.com/kunchenguid/org-bench/pull/364. This should close the last obvious judged gap you asked me to anchor on.
